name: São Jacinto e Estarreja — circular route from Aveiro
name_original: São Jacinto e Estarreja — percurso circular a partir de Aveiro
region: Glória e Glória e Glória e Vera Cruz
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Glória e Glória e Glória e Vera Cruz are primarily situated near Aveiro, Portugal, characterized by flat coastal plains and the extensive lagoon system of the Ria de Aveiro. The terrain features minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for accessible cycling. Routes often follow the coastline, passing through sandy beaches and pine forests, with some trails extending inland through agricultural areas. The region's landscape is shaped by its proximity to the Atlantic Ocean and the unique estuarine environment. Best touring cycling routes around Glória e Glória e Glória e Vera Cruz The most popular touring cycling route is From Aveiro to the Farol da Barra and Praia da Vagueira — circular route, a 28.0 miles (45.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route features coastal views and passes by the Farol da Barra lighthouse. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is From Aveiro to Praia da Vagueira — route in Ria de Aveiro, a moderate 27.8 miles (44.8 km) path. This route offers views of the Ria de Aveiro lagoon and leads to Praia da Vagueira beach. Local touring cyclists also love the From Aveiro to Praia de Mira — circular route by EV1, a 44.8 miles (72.1 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes and along parts of the EuroVelo 1, often completed in about 4 hours 42 minutes. Touring cycling around Glória e Glória e Glória e Vera Cruz is defined by coastal paths, lagoon-side trails, and flat terrain. The network offers options for various ability levels, including 10 easy, 21 moderate, and 6 difficult routes. The routes in Glória e Glória e Glória e Vera Cruz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Glória e Glória e Glória e Vera Cruz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Glória e Glória e Glória e Vera Cruz?
A: There are 37 touring cycling routes available in the region, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. This includes 10 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 6 more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Aveiro?
A: The touring cycling routes around Aveiro are primarily characterized by flat coastal plains and the extensive lagoon system of the Ria de Aveiro. You'll find minimal elevation changes, making for accessible cycling. Routes often follow the coastline, passing through sandy beaches and pine forests, with some trails extending inland through agricultural areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384628298/from-aveiro-to-the-farol-da-barra-and-praia-da-vagueira-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Aveiro to the Farol da Barra and Praia da Vagueira — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 45.1 km long and takes about 2 hours 50 minutes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384638327/from-aveiro-to-praia-de-mira-circular-route-by-ev1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Aveiro to Praia de Mira — circular route by EV1&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 72.1 km trail.

Q: What are some scenic highlights I can see while cycling in the region?
A: The region offers many scenic points. You can cycle past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3795578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costa Nova Striped Houses&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3466981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barra Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6330290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt Pans&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3893934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moliceiro Boats on the Canals of Aveiro&lt;/a&gt; are also a unique sight.

Q: Are there any routes that are part of larger cycling networks?
A: Yes, some routes in the area are part of larger networks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384638327/from-aveiro-to-praia-de-mira-circular-route-by-ev1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Aveiro to Praia de Mira — circular route by EV1&lt;/a&gt; follows parts of the EuroVelo 1, a long-distance cycling route.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes here?
A: The touring cycling routes around Aveiro are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the flat coastal paths, lagoon-side trails, and the variety of options suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Given the predominantly flat terrain and minimal elevation changes, many routes are suitable for families and beginners. There are 10 routes specifically classified as 'easy', providing gentle rides through coastal areas and along the Ria de Aveiro.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Ria de Aveiro lagoon?
A: Absolutely. The region is defined by its proximity to the Ria de Aveiro. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384626504/from-aveiro-to-praia-da-vagueira-route-in-ria-de-aveiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Aveiro to Praia da Vagueira — route in Ria de Aveiro&lt;/a&gt; offer direct views of the lagoon and its unique estuarine environment. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/406382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aveiro Lagoon - Mira Channel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific cycle paths or trails dedicated to cycling?
A: Yes, the area features dedicated cycle paths. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1127649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Mira Cycle Path&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/464903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costa Nova to Vagueira Cycle Path&lt;/a&gt; within the Ria de Aveiro Nature Reserve, offering pleasant and safe cycling experiences.

Q: What natural features can I explore by bike?
A: Beyond the beaches and the lagoon, you can cycle through areas with pine forests and agricultural landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boardwalk through the dunes of Ria de Aveiro&lt;/a&gt; offers a unique perspective on the coastal ecosystem, and you might even pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7050332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pateira de Frossos&lt;/a&gt; lake.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route in the area?
A: One of the longer routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1386843334/eurovelo-1-aveiro-to-mira-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;EuroVelo 1: Aveiro to Mira Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 73.7 km and typically takes around 4 hours and 43 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to specific beaches?
A: Many routes lead to or pass by the region's beautiful beaches. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384628298/from-aveiro-to-the-farol-da-barra-and-praia-da-vagueira-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Aveiro to the Farol da Barra and Praia da Vagueira — circular route&lt;/a&gt; takes you to Praia da Vagueira, known for its coastal charm.
tips: Former Jerónimo Pereira Campos Ceramics Factory, built between 1915 and 1917. The red brick facade and its central chimney are postcards of Aveiro, which highlight the beauty of the canal. It was deactivated in the 1960s and renovated in 1995, becoming the Aveiro Congress Center. Today you can find the IEFP facilities here and some old parts from the factory on display.

The wide Ria de Aveiro lagoon forms a large natural protected port area, into which generations of Portuguese seafaring, trading, and industrial infrastructure have been built over centuries. Bike trails access the historic lighthouse and the ferries to the northern bank of the river

Follow the port road all the way to the end to get to the main ferry across the Ria de Aveiro to Sao Jacinto.  See the photo of the schedule board for timing, and double-check online. If you miss the ferry there are also some other, unfortunately mostly unmarked shuttles a bit earlier along the road. Some of them serve the smaller landing docks around the lagoon, which might at least help to get to the other side.
Cute fishing community on the extreme south end of the narrow sandy peninsula with great bike trails along the waterfront esplanade, dotted with small cafes and restaurants.

The Ria de Aveiro, in Portugal, is an estuary rich in biodiversity. This aquatic ecosystem is famous for its meandering canals, salt marshes and diverse fauna. This point, specifically, is on EN 327, which runs parallel to the Ria for long kilometers, offering incredible views.
Alternative road parallel to EN 109 that offers a few kilometers quieter. Furthermore, it runs through corn fields, making the landscape even more picturesque.

Estarreja is known for the Ria de Aveiro, the annual Carnival and Foral do Antuã of D. Manuel I and for its unique urban art.

At the ESTAU Festival, started by the artist Bordallo II, artists from all over the world transform the city with murals and installations inspired by local culture. In the link below you can find all the murals in Estarreja:
https://www.cm-estarreja.pt/estau
